recommendations.title: File Legal Appeal Against Water Charges Demand
recommendations.description: Initiate filing of a legal appeal against the ₹1,447.34 Cr water charges demand by 2026-05-01, allocating ₹7-10 Cr for legal counsel and expert testimony to mitigate the contingent liability under BRSR:P6:Q14 and GRI:307-1. The Legal Head will oversee this action to avoid crystallization of the liability.
recommendations.profitability_link: Avoid ₹1,447.34 Cr liability, ROI estimated at 14470% (₹1,447.34 Cr / ₹10 Cr legal cost)
recommendations.peer_benchmark: Competitors in power sector have successfully challenged similar demands reducing liabilities by 60-80%

audit_trail.value: JSW Energy faces ₹1,447.34 Cr water charges demand from Chhattisgarh authorities

recommendations.title: Engage Regulators for Water Charge Resolution
recommendations.description: Initiate high-level engagement with Chhattisgarh water authorities by 2026-05-15 to negotiate phased payment or reduction of the ₹1,447.34 Cr demand, supported by ESG and legal teams, aiming to reduce contingent liability and improve regulatory relations.
recommendations.profitability_link: Potential reduction of liability by 10-20%, saving ₹145-290 Cr, ROI 14500%
recommendations.peer_benchmark: Competitors have negotiated regulatory settlements reducing liabilities by up to 25%

recommendations.title: Establish Water Risk Monitoring Dashboard
recommendations.description: Implement a real-time water risk monitoring dashboard by 2026-07-01 to track drought severity (PDSI ≤ -3.0, SPI ≤ -1.5) and flood events (≥ 100-yr ARI or depth ≥ 0.5m), escalating to the Risk Committee if thresholds are breached to enable proactive risk mitigation.
recommendations.profitability_link: Early risk detection can prevent operational disruptions worth ₹50-100 Cr annually, ROI ~200%
recommendations.peer_benchmark: Leading power firms use TNFD-aligned dashboards to reduce water-related outages by 30%

audit_trail.value: Thresholds for severe drought requiring escalation

audit_trail.ref: Top risks: Extreme weather hazard + asset exposure
audit_trail.value: Water scarcity and flood risks critical for JSW Energy
